summary: Reviewed the proposed host-controller spec and architecture-doc changes in the pack. The approach mostly fits the current layering: host-only UI changes stay in the host route, reusable pure copy/logic moves to shared `src/lib`, and public data flow remains through existing display/player paths. The main architectural risk is that display copy extraction is proposed without the display component in the pack, so visual parity and shared-data contract need verification during implementation.
appears_sound: The spec keeps live-game business rules out of the client UI: call-delay persistence and enforcement remain on `game_states.call_delay_seconds` and server actions, with only the host hint removed in `tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md`.
The proposed `HOUSE_RULES` and `getColourName()` helpers are pure shared-library concerns under `src/lib/`, which matches the documented shared-helper pattern in `docs/architecture/relationships.md`.

The proposed first-game check is a server-side Supabase read in `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/page.tsx`, preserving the existing server-component data loading boundary described in `docs/architecture/routes.md`.

findings.description: The spec requires `display-ui.tsx` to switch from inline house-rules JSX to a shared `HOUSE_RULES` constant while promising no public display visual change. Because the display component is not included in the pack, the review cannot verify that the extracted data shape preserves existing icons, line breaks, ordering, keys, and styling contracts.
findings.evidence: The spec says both host briefing and `renderHouseRulesPanel` consume `HOUSE_RULES` and that display styling stays exactly as-is in `tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:134`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If the existing display panel already maps simple icon/text rows, the extraction may be mechanically safe.
findings.what_would_confirm: Review the current `src/app/display/[sessionId]/display-ui.tsx` house-rules JSX before and after implementation, plus a visual screenshot comparison of `/display/[sessionId]`.

findings.description: The mitigation assumes the added first-game query is backed by an index on `(session_id, game_index)`, but the pack's data-model summary does not show indexes. This is unlikely to matter at pub-bingo scale, but the architecture note overstates the performance guarantee unless the schema confirms it.
findings.evidence: The spec claims a `Single indexed read on (session_id, game_index)` in `tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:159`, while `docs/architecture/data-model.md` lists tables and migrations but no index evidence.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: A migration outside the pack may already define the composite index, or the table may be small enough that no index is needed.
findings.what_would_confirm: Check the migrations or schema for an index covering `games(session_id, game_index)`.

empty_categories: No client/server boundary violation found in the proposed host-page data flow.
No data-model or RLS change is proposed, so no schema ownership conflict was found in the pack.
No shared mutable state or duplicate persisted state was introduced by the proposed architecture.
No public route middleware broadening is proposed; display and player auth/performance boundaries remain intact.
